steps.message:
# Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API Migration

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in apache/kafka that implement the Kafka Streams DSL (map, filter, groupBy) and their corresponding Processor API equivalents, to support a team migrating from DSL to lower-level Processor API for custom state store management.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` (apache/kafka)
- `sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` (apache/flink)
- `sg-evals/camel--1006f047` (apache/camel)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find Java source files in apache/kafka that implement the Kafka Streams DSL and their Processor API equivalents. Let me start by exploring the repository structure.

Takes an additional {@link Named} parameter that is used to name the processor in the topology.\n */\n KStream filter(final Predicate predicate, final Named named);\n\n /**\n * Create a new {@code KStream} that consists all records of this stream which do not satisfy the given\n * predicate.\n * All records that do satisfy the predicate are dropped.\n * This is a stateless record-by-record operation (cf. {@link #processValues(FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, String...)}\n * for stateful record processing or if you need access to the record's timestamp, headers, or other metadata).\n *\n * @param predicate\n * a filter {@link Predicate} that is applied to each record\n *\n * @return A {@code KStream} that contains only those records that do not satisfy the given predicate.\n *\n * @see #filter(Predicate)\n */\n KStream filterNot(final Predicate predicate);\n", "numLines": 100, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 1595}}

  "text": "The Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API migration involves understanding how DSL operations like map, filter, and groupBy are implemented using the lower-level Processor API.\n\nCore Processor API Components:\n1. Processor (processor/api/Processor.java) - Base interface for implementing stateless record processing\n2. ProcessorSupplier (processor/api/ProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for creating Processor instances\n3. FixedKeyProcessor (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessor.java) - Processor variant for operations with fixed/unchanged keys\n4. FixedKeyProcessorSupplier (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for FixedKeyProcessor instances\n5. Record (processor/api/Record.java) - Represents individual records with key, value, metadata\n6. ContextualProcessor & ContextualFixedKeyProcessor - Base classes that provide context access\n\nDSL Operation Implementations:\nEach DSL operation is implemented as a ProcessorSupplier that creates Processor instances:\n\n1. Filter Operations:\n   - KStreamFilter (kstream/internals/KStreamFilter.java) - Implements FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, uses ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n   - KTableFilter (kstream/internals/KTableFilter.java) - For table-based filtering\n   - Uses Predicate interface for filtering logic\n\n2. Map Operations:\n   - KStreamMap (kstream/internals/KStreamMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for key-value mapping\n   - KStreamMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamMapValues.java) - Maps only values, preserves keys (FixedKeyProcessorSupplier)\n   - KTableMapValues (kstream/internals/KTableMapValues.java) - Table variant\n   - Uses KeyValueMapper for transformation\n\n3. FlatMap Operations:\n   - KStreamFlatMap (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for flattening key-value pairs\n   - KStreamFlatMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMapValues.java) - Flattens values only\n   - Returns Iterable<KeyValue<K, V>> for multiple output records per input\n\n4. Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
}

  "text": "The Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API migration involves understanding how DSL operations like map, filter, and groupBy are implemented using the lower-level Processor API.\n\nCore Processor API Components:\n1. Processor (processor/api/Processor.java) - Base interface for implementing stateless record processing\n2. ProcessorSupplier (processor/api/ProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for creating Processor instances\n3. FixedKeyProcessor (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessor.java) - Processor variant for operations with fixed/unchanged keys\n4. FixedKeyProcessorSupplier (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for FixedKeyProcessor instances\n5. Record (processor/api/Record.java) - Represents individual records with key, value, metadata\n6. ContextualProcessor & ContextualFixedKeyProcessor - Base classes that provide context access\n\nDSL Operation Implementations:\nEach DSL operation is implemented as a ProcessorSupplier that creates Processor instances:\n\n1. Filter Operations:\n   - KStreamFilter (kstream/internals/KStreamFilter.java) - Implements FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, uses ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n   - KTableFilter (kstream/internals/KTableFilter.java) - For table-based filtering\n   - Uses Predicate interface for filtering logic\n\n2. Map Operations:\n   - KStreamMap (kstream/internals/KStreamMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for key-value mapping\n   - KStreamMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamMapValues.java) - Maps only values, preserves keys (FixedKeyProcessorSupplier)\n   - KTableMapValues (kstream/internals/KTableMapValues.java) - Table variant\n   - Uses KeyValueMapper for transformation\n\n3. FlatMap Operations:\n   - KStreamFlatMap (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for flattening key-value pairs\n   - KStreamFlatMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMapValues.java) - Flattens values only\n   - Returns Iterable<KeyValue<K, V>> for multiple output records per input\n\n4. Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
}

GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()).   "text": "The Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API migration involves understanding how DSL operations like map, filter, and groupBy are implemented using the lower-level Processor API.\n\nCore Processor API Components:\n1. Processor (processor/api/Processor.java) - Base interface for implementing stateless record processing\n2. ProcessorSupplier (processor/api/ProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for creating Processor instances\n3. FixedKeyProcessor (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessor.java) - Processor variant for operations with fixed/unchanged keys\n4. FixedKeyProcessorSupplier (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for FixedKeyProcessor instances\n5. Record (processor/api/Record.java) - Represents individual records with key, value, metadata\n6. ContextualProcessor & ContextualFixedKeyProcessor - Base classes that provide context access\n\nDSL Operation Implementations:\nEach DSL operation is implemented as a ProcessorSupplier that creates Processor instances:\n\n1. Filter Operations:\n   - KStreamFilter (kstream/internals/KStreamFilter.java) - Implements FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, uses ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n   - KTableFilter (kstream/internals/KTableFilter.java) - For table-based filtering\n   - Uses Predicate interface for filtering logic\n\n2. Map Operations:\n   - KStreamMap (kstream/internals/KStreamMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for key-value mapping\n   - KStreamMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamMapValues.java) - Maps only values, preserves keys (FixedKeyProcessorSupplier)\n   - KTableMapValues (kstream/internals/KTableMapValues.java) - Table variant\n   - Uses KeyValueMapper for transformation\n\n3. FlatMap Operations:\n   - KStreamFlatMap (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for flattening key-value pairs\n   - KStreamFlatMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMapValues.java) - Flattens values only\n   - Returns Iterable<KeyValue<K, V>> for multiple output records per input\n\n4. Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
}

  "text": "The Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API migration involves understanding how DSL operations like map, filter, and groupBy are implemented using the lower-level Processor API.\n\nCore Processor API Components:\n1. Processor (processor/api/Processor.java) - Base interface for implementing stateless record processing\n2. ProcessorSupplier (processor/api/ProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for creating Processor instances\n3. FixedKeyProcessor (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessor.java) - Processor variant for operations with fixed/unchanged keys\n4. FixedKeyProcessorSupplier (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for FixedKeyProcessor instances\n5. Record (processor/api/Record.java) - Represents individual records with key, value, metadata\n6. ContextualProcessor & ContextualFixedKeyProcessor - Base classes that provide context access\n\nDSL Operation Implementations:\nEach DSL operation is implemented as a ProcessorSupplier that creates Processor instances:\n\n1. Filter Operations:\n   - KStreamFilter (kstream/internals/KStreamFilter.java) - Implements FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, uses ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n   - KTableFilter (kstream/internals/KTableFilter.java) - For table-based filtering\n   - Uses Predicate interface for filtering logic\n\n2. Map Operations:\n   - KStreamMap (kstream/internals/KStreamMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for key-value mapping\n   - KStreamMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamMapValues.java) - Maps only values, preserves keys (FixedKeyProcessorSupplier)\n   - KTableMapValues (kstream/internals/KTableMapValues.java) - Table variant\n   - Uses KeyValueMapper for transformation\n\n3. FlatMap Operations:\n   - KStreamFlatMap (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for flattening key-value pairs\n   - KStreamFlatMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMapValues.java) - Flattens values only\n   - Returns Iterable<KeyValue<K, V>> for multiple output records per input\n\n4. Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
}

Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
}

  "text": "The Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API migration involves understanding how DSL operations like map, filter, and groupBy are implemented using the lower-level Processor API.\n\nCore Processor API Components:\n1. Processor (processor/api/Processor.java) - Base interface for implementing stateless record processing\n2. ProcessorSupplier (processor/api/ProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for creating Processor instances\n3. FixedKeyProcessor (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessor.java) - Processor variant for operations with fixed/unchanged keys\n4. FixedKeyProcessorSupplier (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for FixedKeyProcessor instances\n5. Record (processor/api/Record.java) - Represents individual records with key, value, metadata\n6. ContextualProcessor & ContextualFixedKeyProcessor - Base classes that provide context access\n\nDSL Operation Implementations:\nEach DSL operation is implemented as a ProcessorSupplier that creates Processor instances:\n\n1. Filter Operations:\n   - KStreamFilter (kstream/internals/KStreamFilter.java) - Implements FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, uses ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n   - KTableFilter (kstream/internals/KTableFilter.java) - For table-based filtering\n   - Uses Predicate interface for filtering logic\n\n2. Map Operations:\n   - KStreamMap (kstream/internals/KStreamMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for key-value mapping\n   - KStreamMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamMapValues.java) - Maps only values, preserves keys (FixedKeyProcessorSupplier)\n   - KTableMapValues (kstream/internals/KTableMapValues.java) - Table variant\n   - Uses KeyValueMapper for transformation\n\n3. FlatMap Operations:\n   - KStreamFlatMap (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for flattening key-value pairs\n   - KStreamFlatMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMapValues.java) - Flattens values only\n   - Returns Iterable<KeyValue<K, V>> for multiple output records per input\n\n4. Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
}

  "text": "The Kafka Streams DSL to Processor API migration involves understanding how DSL operations like map, filter, and groupBy are implemented using the lower-level Processor API.\n\nCore Processor API Components:\n1. Processor (processor/api/Processor.java) - Base interface for implementing stateless record processing\n2. ProcessorSupplier (processor/api/ProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for creating Processor instances\n3. FixedKeyProcessor (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessor.java) - Processor variant for operations with fixed/unchanged keys\n4. FixedKeyProcessorSupplier (processor/api/FixedKeyProcessorSupplier.java) - Factory for FixedKeyProcessor instances\n5. Record (processor/api/Record.java) - Represents individual records with key, value, metadata\n6. ContextualProcessor & ContextualFixedKeyProcessor - Base classes that provide context access\n\nDSL Operation Implementations:\nEach DSL operation is implemented as a ProcessorSupplier that creates Processor instances:\n\n1. Filter Operations:\n   - KStreamFilter (kstream/internals/KStreamFilter.java) - Implements FixedKeyProcessorSupplier, uses ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n   - KTableFilter (kstream/internals/KTableFilter.java) - For table-based filtering\n   - Uses Predicate interface for filtering logic\n\n2. Map Operations:\n   - KStreamMap (kstream/internals/KStreamMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for key-value mapping\n   - KStreamMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamMapValues.java) - Maps only values, preserves keys (FixedKeyProcessorSupplier)\n   - KTableMapValues (kstream/internals/KTableMapValues.java) - Table variant\n   - Uses KeyValueMapper for transformation\n\n3. FlatMap Operations:\n   - KStreamFlatMap (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMap.java) - Implements ProcessorSupplier for flattening key-value pairs\n   - KStreamFlatMapValues (kstream/internals/KStreamFlatMapValues.java) - Flattens values only\n   - Returns Iterable<KeyValue<K, V>> for multiple output records per input\n\n4. Aggregation Operations:\n   - KStreamAggregate (kstream/internals/KStreamAggregate.java) - ProcessorSupplier for stream aggregation\n   - KStreamReduce (kstream/internals/KStreamReduce.java) - Reducer implementation\n   - KStreamWindowAggregate - Windowed aggregation variant\n   - KTableAggregate, KTableReduce - Table aggregation variants\n   - Use Initializer, Aggregator, and Reducer interfaces\n\n5. GroupBy Operations:\n   - KGroupedStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedStreamImpl.java) - Represents grouped streams\n   - KGroupedTableImpl (kstream/internals/KGroupedTableImpl.java) - Represents grouped tables\n   - Requires repartitioning with KStreamRepartitionMap\n\nDSL Interface Classes:\n- KStream (kstream/KStream.java) - Main DSL interface defining filter, map, flatMap, groupBy operations\n- KTable (kstream/KTable.java) - Table DSL interface\n- KGroupedStream (kstream/KGroupedStream.java) - Grouped stream interface\n- KGroupedTable (kstream/KGroupedTable.java) - Grouped table interface\n\nImplementation Classes:\n- KStreamImpl (kstream/internals/KStreamImpl.java) - Implements KStream interface, creates ProcessorSupplier instances\n- KTableImpl (kstream/internals/KTableImpl.java) - Implements KTable interface\n- These classes use InternalStreamsBuilder to add processor nodes to topology\n\nKey Pattern:\nDSL methods create ProcessorGraphNode instances with ProcessorParameters containing the appropriate ProcessorSupplier (e.g., new KStreamFilter(), new KStreamMap()). The topology builder integrates these into the execution graph. The ProcessorSupplier.get() method returns a Processor instance that extends ContextualProcessor or ContextualFixedKeyProcessor, implementing the actual processing logic via the process() method.\n\nFor custom state store management during migration, users can:\n1. Replace DSL operations with Processor API using ProcessorSupplier\n2. Implement custom Processor classes extending ContextualProcessor/ContextualFixedKeyProcessor\n3. Access ProcessorContext for state store management\n4. Use Topology.addProcessor() or StreamsBuilder.addStateStore() for state store configuration\n\nRelated Resources:\n- StreamsBuilder (StreamsBuilder.java) - Utility for building topologies with DSL\n- Topology (Topology.java) - Lower-level topology definition using Processor API\n- ProcessorContext implementations handle record forwarding and state store access"
